content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

February 18, 1977          A-77-
                                    318

W. A. McCormick, Controller
The Republican Central Committee
of Los Angeles County
619 South New Hampshire Avenue
Los Angeles, CA  90005

Dear Mr. McCormick:

     Kenneth Finney has asked me to respond to your letters of November 24, 1976. Although this office has no record of receiving the November correspondence, we have received your subsequent letter dated January 25, 1977, and the enclosed copies of your earlier letters.

     With regard to your question concerning the receipt of funds from the Republican National Committee, the $30,000 payment was a late contribution and should have been disclosed as such. Furthermore, the contribution should have been disclosed on the postelection statement filed by the Republican Central Committee.

     You also asked whether dues payments of $1,000 or more that are received by several committees after the closing date of the last preelection statement are "late contributions." As Mr. Finney indicated in a telephone conversation on November 23, 1976, the Political Reform Act does not distinguish between types of contributions when it imposes reporting requirements applicable to late contributions. Consequently, if the dues payments described above are "contributions" within the meaning of Government Code Section 82015, they are late contributions and you must comply with the requirements of Section 84214. In addition, you should have reported receipt of the dues payments on the postelection statements filed by each of the committees in question.

     As you are aware, you have violated the disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act by failing to report the contribution from the Republican National Committee and certain dues


TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION  915 322 5660  •  EXECUTIVE LEGAL  322 5901  •  ENFORCEMENT CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322 6441

W. A. McCormick
Page Two
February 17, 1977


receipts as "late contributions."  However, under the circum-
stances as outlined by you in your letters and telephone con-
versations with Mr. Finney, it is doubtful that the Commission
will initiate an enforcement proceeding so long as the com-
mittees' postelection statements fully and accurately disclose
receipt of these contributions.  If the postelection statements
do not make such disclosure, you should amend them immediately.

I hope this information will be of assistance.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Natalie E. West
                                        Staff Counsel
                                        Legal Division

NEW:jo

THE REPUBLICAN
Central Committee of Los Angeles County

J WILLIAM OROZCO
CHAIRMAN

619 SOUTH NEW [illegible] ST
LOS ANGELES CALIFORNIA 90015

24 November 1976

Kenneth Finney, Legal Staff
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 907
Sacramento, Ca., 95804

Dear Mr. Finney,

On November 1 the Republican Central Committee of Los Angeles County, IDE 742145,
received $ 30,000 from the Republican National Committee. The check from the Republican National Committee was dated October 26 and the description on the bottom of the check was "Transfer Out".

Because the Republican Central Committee of Los Angeles County is not a reporting
committee with the Federal Election Commission and because the funds received were
on share of net proceeds of the Republican National Committee's October 7 fund
raiser in Beverly Hills, I called on November 2 and received a callback on November
4 from Rodney Smith of the Republican National Committee. He advised me that the
IRS terminology would stand. I had previously contacted the Federal Election Commission on November 2 and asked the legal staff to make a determination whether we
should make a report of the receipt even when we had not been subject to reporting
under the act.

On November 9 the Federal Election Commission advised me that this committee would
not be required to make a report. However, it was too late to qualify under
Proposition Nine for reporting within 48 hours. Would you please clarify this
matter for the future?

In the past and for the present, the Republican Central Committee of Los Angeles
County asks that the Republican State Central Committee, the Republican National
Committee, or any other committee not headquartered in Los Angeles County, share
list net proceeds with this committee when holding a fund raiser within the county.
We can not anticipate the amount because each is based upon a percentage of net
proceeds.

We appreciate your prompt reply to this inquiry.

Sincerely,

[signature]
W.A. McCormick, Controller

W.A.: hs

THE REPUBLICAN
Central Committee of Los Angeles County

J. WILLIAM OROZCO
CHAIRMAN                                381-6081
                                    619 SOUTH NEW HAMPSHIRE AVENUE
                                    LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90005

                                    24 November 1976


Kenneth Finney, Legal Staff
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 007
Sacramento, Ca., 95004

Dear Mr. Finney,

May I, on behalf of the Republican Central Committee of Los Angeles County and
of the Committee to Elect State and Local Candidates, of which I am controller,
and the Lincoln Clubs of Los Angeles County, of which I am treasurer, ask for a
clarification of the reporting rules of $1,000 or more contributions received
during the days immediately prior to an election?

Both the Republican Central Committee and the Committee to Elect State and Local
Candidates have unaffiliated Statesmen memberships recorded by them throughout the
year. We have 25,000 memberships and 3,000 memberships. If the person or the
organization chooses to pay quarterly or semi-annually, the amount received will
exceed $1,000 but has nothing to do with the election. Must such a payment be re-
ported within the required 48 hours?

Similarly, the Lincoln Clubs of Los Angeles County has 1,000 per year memberships
which we can not anticipate when the member will send in his money. Do these fall
within the 40 hour reporting requirement?

                                    Sincerely,

                                    [signature]
                                    W.A. McCormick
